method: Tier-0 sweep WebFetch of primary Commission page, cross-check WebFetch of the Commission's guidelines library page, adversarial search pass for independent corroboration and to check for disputes/errors in dates or scope
origin_items: European Commission Digital Strategy News (Tier 0 html sweep) -- guidelines on transparency obligations for providers and deployers of certain AI systems

dedup_check.query: European Commission publishes AI transparency obligation guidelines
dedup_check.result: one moderate match: eu-ai-cybersecurity-action-plan (2026-07-09), a different Commission action (cybersecurity, not Article 50 transparency) -- read before deciding; not the same event or saga, so treated as standalone rather than update/follow-up

claims.text: On 20 July 2026 the Commission published guidelines on transparency obligations for providers and deployers of certain AI systems under Article 50 of the AI Act
claims.reasoning: Confirmed directly by the Commission's own news page (Tier 0) and its guidelines library page (Tier 0, same institutional origin, independently indexed by web search).

claims.text: Guidelines are non-binding practical guidance, not new legislation
claims.reasoning: Tier-0 self-description; consistent with the AI Act's general framework for Commission guidelines under Art. 96.

claims.text: Provider and deployer obligations as described (informing users, machine-readable marks, deepfake/public-interest-content/emotion-biometric disclosure)
claims.reasoning: Directly stated on the Tier-0 primary page; matches the general text of AI Act Article 50, which the adversarial search pass located independently (artificialintelligenceact.eu reference text for Article 50).

claims.text: Article 50 transparency obligations begin applying on 2 August 2026
claims.reasoning: Stated on the Tier-0 page and independently corroborated by the adversarial search pass, which found the same 2 August 2026 date on the Commission's guidelines library page.

notes: Single-topic announcement covered entirely by Commission-owned Tier-0 pages, which is sufficient per verification.md Sec.3 for announcement-type claims. Adversarial pass also surfaced a companion Code of Practice on Transparency of AI-Generated Content and a stakeholder signatory deadline of 18:00 CET 22 July 2026 -- left out of the published article as tangential detail, not core to the guidelines announcement itself. No disputing or contradicting coverage found.
